summary: Iteration 7 verified: (1) DELETE /api/community/posts/{post_id} works with 200/403/404/auth-required; (2) Community page delete button only renders for authored posts; (3) Posts include user_id on create/list; (4) Interview prompts (build_system_prompt, next-round transition, end-interview report) contain BRUTALLY HONEST personality + low-score guidance; (5) AIInterview shows IDE for both Round 1 and Round 2.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 918 lines - single-file monolith. Recommend splitting into modules (interviews, community, problems, auth) per >700-line guideline.
DELETE /community/posts/{post_id}: post.get('user_id') comparison is correct, but 403 message could be standardized; minor only.
Community.jsx PostCard handleDelete uses window.confirm() (browser native dialog). Works in tests via dialog.accept() but a Shadcn AlertDialog would be more on-brand and testable.
next-round transition prompt is built inline (8 lines of text). Could be moved into a constants section for easier prompt-engineering iteration.
end-interview report prompt is a single 1.2KB string on one line (line 339). Hard to read/maintain - extract to a constant.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (community delete UI verified end-to-end; IDE round-2 verified via code inspection)

seed_data_creation: Tests created TEST_ prefixed posts via POST and cleaned them up via DELETE. The 403 test inserts a foreign-user post directly into MongoDB and removes it in finally.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 7 features all verified. Backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration7.py (11 tests). Test classes: TestCommunityCreateUserId, TestCommunityDelete (own/404/403/auth), TestHonestPromptContent (3 prompts), TestAIInterviewIDE, TestCommunityFrontendDelete. Frontend verification: navigated to /community with session_token cookie, observed 11 delete buttons on Test Developer's posts and ZERO on Yash Gavas' post (different user); clicked delete -> count 11->10, post removed from DOM. The IDE round-2 toggle was verified via file inspection (live interview test would consume LLM credits per project guidance).

features_tested.community_delete_own_post: PASS - returns 200 {deleted: true}, post removed from DB
features_tested.community_delete_403_others_post: PASS - returns 403 when user_id mismatch (verified via direct mongo insert)
features_tested.community_post_has_user_id: PASS - POST returns user_id, GET list items contain user_id
features_tested.community_ui_delete_button_authored_only: PASS - 11 delete buttons visible only on Test Developer's posts; Yash Gavas' post has no trash icon (visual confirmation in screenshot)
features_tested.community_ui_delete_action: PASS - clicking trash deletes post, count 11->10, DOM updated
features_tested.interview_system_prompt_brutally_honest: PASS - 'BRUTALLY HONEST', 'NOT a cheerleader', 'FAANG interviewer', 'FORBIDDEN' generic praise rules present
features_tested.interview_next_round_honest_assessment: PASS - transition prompt includes 'BRUTALLY HONEST assessment' and 'Strong / Adequate / Needs Improvement / Poor' rating
features_tested.interview_end_report_honest: PASS - 'BRUTALLY HONEST interview evaluator', 'below 40' guideline for poor DSA, 'Do NOT inflate scores' present
features_tested.aiinterview_ide_round_1_and_2: PASS - useEffect uses (round === 1 || round === 2) and toggle button uses (currentRound === 1 || currentRound === 2); data-testid='toggle-ide-btn' present

rca_of_the_issue: Not a bug iteration - feature additions verified successfully. The delete button visibility logic at Community.jsx:45 'currentUserId && post.user_id === currentUserId' is the correct gating, and the backend DELETE endpoint enforces ownership at server.py:827.
